detaileddescription.para: Generated from class MR::MeshMeshDistanceResult. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_MeshMeshDistanceResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_MeshMeshDistanceResult_DestroyArray(). Use MR_MeshMeshDistanceResult_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_MeshMeshDistanceResult_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Constructs MR::MeshMeshDistanceResult elementwise. Parameter a can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter a might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter b can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter b might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_MeshMeshDistanceResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_MeshPart *a, const MR_MeshPart *b, const MR_AffineXf3f *rigidB2A, const float *upDistLimitSq)
memberdef.name: MR_findDistance

briefdescription.para: computes minimal distance between two meshes or two mesh regions

parameterdescription.para: rigid transformation from B-mesh space to A mesh space, nullptr considered as identity transformation

upper limit on the distance in question, if the real distance is larger then the function exists returning upDistLimitSq and no valid points Generated from function MR::findDistance. Parameter a can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter b can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rigidB2A defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter upDistLimitSq has a default argument: 3.40282347e38f, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_MeshMeshDistanceResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_MeshPart *a, const MR_MeshPart *b, const MR_AffineXf3f *rigidB2A, const float *upDistLimitSq)
memberdef.name: MR_findSignedDistance_MR_MeshPart

briefdescription.para: computes minimal distance between two meshes

parameterdescription.para: rigid transformation from B-mesh space to A mesh space, nullptr considered as identity transformation

upper limit on the positive distance in question, if the real distance is larger then the function exists returning upDistLimitSq and no valid points Generated from function MR::findSignedDistance. Parameter a can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter b can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rigidB2A defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter upDistLimitSq has a default argument: 3.40282347e38f, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_MeshMeshSignedDistanceResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_MeshPart *a, const MR_MeshPart *b, const MR_AffineXf3f *rigidB2A, const float *maxDistanceSq)
memberdef.name: MR_findMaxDistanceSqOneWay_MR_MeshPart

briefdescription.para: returns the maximum of the squared distances from each B-mesh vertex to A-mesh

parameterdescription.para: rigid transformation from B-mesh space to A mesh space, nullptr considered as identity transformation

upper limit on the positive distance in question, if the real distance is larger than the function exists returning maxDistanceSq Generated from function MR::findMaxDistanceSqOneWay. Parameter a can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter b can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rigidB2A defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter maxDistanceSq has a default argument: 3.40282347e38f, pass a null pointer to use it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_MeshPart *a, const MR_MeshPart *b, const MR_AffineXf3f *rigidB2A, const float *maxDistanceSq)
memberdef.name: MR_findMaxDistanceSq_MR_MeshPart

briefdescription.para: returns the squared Hausdorff distance between two meshes, that is the maximum of squared distances from each mesh vertex to the other mesh (in both directions)

parameterdescription.para: rigid transformation from B-mesh space to A mesh space, nullptr considered as identity transformation

upper limit on the positive distance in question, if the real distance is larger than the function exists returning maxDistanceSq Generated from function MR::findMaxDistanceSq. Parameter a can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter b can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rigidB2A defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter maxDistanceSq has a default argument: 3.40282347e38f, pass a null pointer to use it.
